# CoveN AI — ACO Explainer

> Agent Citability Optimisation (ACO) is the practice of making websites visible, trustworthy, and structured for AI agents.

This page explains what ACO is, how it is scored, and how it differs from SEO. It is maintained by CoveN AI, a platform that measures and improves AI agent citability for websites.

## Key concepts

- **ACO (Agent Citability Optimisation)** — optimising a site so AI agents (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, Gemini) cite, reference, and return it when users ask relevant questions
- **ACO score** — a 0–100 score across five dimensions: discoverability, structure, trust signals, agent interface, and correlation
- **ACI (Agent Citation Index)** — CoveN AI's ranked index of which sites agents are actually citing
- **llms.txt** — a plain-text file at /llms.txt that gives AI agents a direct, machine-readable summary of what a site contains

## Free tools

- Free citability scan: https://aa.covenai.io
- Scan any URL, get a full ACO score and prioritised recommendations

## About CoveN AI

- Website: https://covenai.io
- Agent Analytics platform: https://aa.covenai.io
- Founded to build the measurement and optimisation layer for the agentic web
